title: Conditionals (Type 0, 1 & 2) — Second Conditional
objectives: Hiểu và áp dụng: second conditional
Học 24 từ vựng theo chủ đề tuần 8
Hoàn thành 40 câu hỏi trắc nghiệm tăng dần độ khó
Bám sát mục tiêu tuần: Conditionals (Type 0, 1 & 2)
lesson: **Conditionals (Type 0, 1 and 2).** Conditional sentences describe a **condition** and its **result**. The **zero conditional** (if + present, present) states facts: 'If you heat ice, it *melts*.' The **first conditional** (if + present, will + verb) talks about real future possibilities: 'If it *rains*, we *will stay* home.' The **second conditional** (if + past, would + verb) describes **unreal** or **hypothetical** situations: 'If I *were* rich, I *would* travel.' Note that *were* is used for all subjects in the second conditional. *Unless* means 'if not'. Think about **consequences**: what happens if water boils, what will you do if it rains tomorrow, and what would you do if you won the lottery?
